evals.prompt: Upgrade my ID Capture integration to the latest SDK (v8). It stopped compiling. Here's my screen file: before-v7-idcapture.dart and pubspec_v7.yaml.
evals.expected_output: The skill migrates the v7 scanner assignment (settings.scannerType = FullDocumentScanner()) to the v8 wrapped form (settings.scanner = IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocumentScanner: FullDocumentScanner())), replaces the removed AamvaBarcodeVerifier with settings.rejectForgedAamvaBarcodes + reading verificationResult.aamvaBarcodeVerification, bumps pubspec to 8.x, and adds the AAMVA add-on package. Listener and lifecycle are preserved.

assertions.text: settings.scannerType is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)
verificationResult.aamvaBarcodeVerification is read instead of calling a verifier (semantic)
AamvaBarcodeVerifier is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)
AamvaBarcodeVerifier.create is not called in the final migrated code, though it may appear in before/after explanation prose (semantic)
pubspec is bumped to an 8.x version for the scandit packages (semantic)
the existing didCaptureId and didRejectId callbacks are preserved (semantic)

evals.prompt: Just fix the scanner API in this file for the v8 upgrade — I'll deal with the rest later. Here's before-v7-idcapture.dart.
evals.expected_output: The skill changes settings.scannerType = FullDocumentScanner() into settings.scanner = IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocumentScanner: FullDocumentScanner()).

assertions.text: settings.scannerType is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)

evals.prompt: After upgrading to v8 I also want to reject expired documents and anyone under 18, using the new declarative settings rather than checking manually. Here's before-v7-idcapture.dart.
evals.expected_output: The skill completes the v8 scanner/verifier migration and additionally sets settings.rejectExpiredIds = true and settings.rejectHolderBelowAge = 18, noting these surface documentExpired and holderUnderage in didRejectId.

assertions.text: these flags are described as surfacing reasons in didRejectId, e.g. documentExpired and holderUnderage (semantic)
settings.scannerType is no longer used in the migrated code (semantic)

evals.prompt: This old ID Capture screen uses supportedDocuments and supportedSides and won't compile anymore. Migrate it to the current API. Here's before-v6-idcapture.dart.
evals.expected_output: The skill replaces the removed v6 supportedDocuments bitmask with a list-based settings.acceptedDocuments of document classes (IdCard, Passport), replaces supportedSides with a scanner set via settings.scanner = IdCaptureScanner(physicalDocumentScanner: FullDocumentScanner()), and replaces capturedId.documentType with capturedId.document?.documentType. No supportedDocuments, supportedSides, IdDocumentType, or capturedId.documentType remain in the migrated code.

assertions.text: settings.acceptedDocuments is configured with document objects like IdCard and Passport (semantic)
supportedDocuments is not used in executable migrated code (explanatory comments may reference it) (semantic)
supportedSides is not used in executable migrated code (explanatory comments may reference it) (semantic)
IdDocumentType is not used in executable migrated code (explanatory comments may reference it) (semantic)
the document type is read via capturedId.document?.documentType, not capturedId.documentType, in the migrated code blocks (semantic)
